title: cerastium pumilum subsp. pallens
intro: Cerastium pumilum subsp. pallens, commonly known as the alumroot or dusty miller, is a flowering plant species in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Cerastium pumilum subsp. pallens include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as an antimicrobial agent, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as to promote overall well-being.
